codeReview.summary: 32 well-structured tests covering all 9 exported StreakTracker methods and all 22 validation assertions (VAL-STREAK-001 through VAL-STREAK-022). Tests use proper isolation (localStorage.clear in beforeEach), fake timers for date-dependent logic, and good assertion quality with toEqual, toBe, toBeCloseTo, and toHaveLength. Code follows established project conventions from AGENTS.md and the test-worker skill.

issues.description: The history trim test creates 365 entries from (i=365 down to 1), which are 365 days before 2025-01-15 through 1 day before. After recording activity on 2025-01-15 (day 0), the history becomes 366 entries, then trimmed to 365. The test verifies length is 365 and that the last entry is today, but does not verify that the oldest entry was correctly trimmed (the comment says 'original first entry was trimmed' but no assertion checks this). The test still validates the core behavior (cap at 365, newest entry present).

summary: Comprehensive test coverage for streakTracker.js with 32 tests covering all 22 validation assertions. Tests are well-organized by method, use fake timers correctly for date-dependent logic, and follow all project conventions. One minor non-blocking observation: the history trim test could more precisely assert which entry was trimmed. All tests pass, lint is clean, and the full suite (196 tests) passes.
